attributes.description: Physical endurance, raw strength, the ability to keep going when ship systems fail and the air goes thin. Not athleticism for its own sake — it's the willingness to hold on.
examples: wrenching a stuck airlock open before the breach widens
carrying a wounded crewmate down a flooded corridor
fighting through hull-rash and oxygen debt

attributes.description: Speed, precision, fine motor control. Pilot's hands, gunner's eye, surgeon's stitch.
examples: threading a freighter through an asteroid swarm
shooting first when a deal goes sideways
patching a leaking suit during EVA

attributes.description: Engineering instinct and applied knowledge. Reading a system's faults, jury-rigging repairs, recognizing patterns in unfamiliar machinery.
examples: rerouting power around a damaged junction
improvising a repair with mismatched parts

attributes.description: Composure under pressure and resistance to fear. Holding your line when the cargo bay is rattling and the pirates are hailing.
examples: staying calm when alien tech starts singing back
facing down an angry station boss
lying convincingly to a customs officer

attributes.description: Social navigation in a multicultural frontier — persuasion, deception, leadership, the ability to make a stranger want to help you.
examples: talking a hostile crew into standing down
negotiating a salvage rate up by twenty percent
gathering rumor in a dockside cantina

attributes.description: Frontier instinct — the prickle that says don't go in there, the read on a scene that doesn't add up, sensitivity to the deep-dark wrongness of alien things.
examples: sensing an ambush before it springs
catching the off-pattern in alien glyphs
knowing which rumor is the truthful one
